problem_classes.name: Phantom Dependencies & Imports
problem_classes.description: Code references packages, modules, or APIs that don't exist
examples: import from package not in requirements.txt
calling API endpoint that was never built
referencing env var that's not set
problem_classes.detection: Import resolution check, requirements.txt vs actual imports, env var cross-reference

problem_classes.name: Invented API Contracts
problem_classes.description: Code calls external services with parameters or endpoints that don't match reality
examples: Stripe API call with wrong parameter name
webhook handler expecting fields the provider doesn't send
SDK method that doesn't exist in the installed version
problem_classes.detection: API contract validation against docs/SDKs, response schema verification, version-specific method check

problem_classes.name: False Assumptions in Logic
problem_classes.description: Code assumes things about data, state, or behavior that aren't true
problem_classes.detection: Assumption enumeration, null/undefined path analysis, implicit contract audit

problem_classes.name: Stale or Fictional Documentation
problem_classes.description: Docs, comments, or README describe behavior that doesn't match the code
examples: README says 'run npm start' but package.json has no start script
docstring describes parameters the function doesn't accept
problem_classes.detection: Doc-code cross-reference, command verification, module existence check

problem_classes.name: Inconsistent Data Models
problem_classes.description: Data shapes assumed by different parts of the code don't match
examples: migration adds field but model doesn't reference it
type definition doesn't match runtime shape
problem_classes.detection: Type/schema consistency check, frontend-backend contract comparison, migration-model alignment

problem_classes.name: Orphaned & Dead Code
problem_classes.description: Code exists that serves no purpose, confusing future AI sessions
problem_classes.detection: Dead code analysis, unused export detection, unreachable code identification

on_stale.message: Anti-hallucination program has not run in {days} days. AI-generated code is accumulating without verification.

problem_classes.name: Decision Traceability
problem_classes.description: Important decisions are recorded with context and rationale
examples: why we use X instead of Y is tribal knowledge
problem_classes.detection: Decision log review, git history scan for large changes without ADR, implicit decision detection

problem_classes.name: System Model Currency
problem_classes.description: State files, invariants, and constraints reflect actual code
examples: state.md says 'pre-launch' but app is live
problem_classes.detection: System file cross-reference against code, state vs reality check

problem_classes.name: Documentation Freshness
problem_classes.description: Docs and comments match current behavior
problem_classes.detection: Doc-code divergence scan, command verification, setup step testing

problem_classes.name: Session Context Reliability
problem_classes.description: What the AI reads at session start is accurate and complete
examples: session handoff notes reference completed work as pending
problem_classes.detection: Workstream file audit, handoff note verification, queue currency check

on_stale.message: Knowledge integrity program has not been checked in {days} days.

problem_classes.name: Claim Verification
problem_classes.description: Every domain claim the product makes can be traced to an authorized source
examples: clinical claim in UI without citation to peer-reviewed or textbook source
financial number in report that wasn't computed from verified ground truth
scientific derivation presented as proof with no SymPy or reviewed-paper anchor
problem_classes.detection: Claim inventory cross-referenced against evidence-standards-matrix.md; any ungrounded claim is a finding

problem_classes.name: Evidence Chain Integrity
problem_classes.description: Each claim's evidence chain (data -> analysis -> claim) is intact and inspectable
examples: claim references a paper that has since been retracted
internal derivation lost its SymPy verification when the file was refactored
anatomical value in code has no source field linking back to an authorized reference
problem_classes.detection: Evidence freshness audit, source-reference integrity check, re-verification cadence review

problem_classes.name: Claim-Backing Invariants
problem_classes.description: Rules that MUST hold if the product is making claims in this domain are enforced in code
examples: clinical claim domain requires every generated image to cite its primitive sources - invariant missing
financial claim domain requires every computed total to trace to line items - invariant missing
scientific claim domain requires every derivation to pass SymPy verification - invariant missing
problem_classes.detection: Invariant enumeration per claim domain, code-path coverage vs. claims made, failure-mode enumeration

problem_classes.name: Evaluator Ground Truth (AI-as-judge setups only)
problem_classes.description: If an AI evaluator grades outputs before release, its judgments are anchored to truth (not just founder agreement)
examples: AISimPT-style evaluator drifts from real clinician review; calibration is self-referential
rubric grader trained on founder-approved examples gradually encodes founder biases
no scheduled human-expert spot-check cadence, so evaluator-truth divergence can compound silently
problem_classes.detection: Evaluator validation plan audit, spot-check cadence review, divergence metric check

problem_classes.name: Disclaimer & Scope Boundaries
problem_classes.description: The product is explicit about what it does NOT claim; readers can't be misled
examples: research tool used in clinical context without disclaimer
pre-FDA device claims that imply clearance
v1 scope leaks into v2/v3 implied capability in marketing or docs
problem_classes.detection: Surface audit across README, UI copy, docs; scope-fence cross-reference

on_stale.message: Claims Policy program has not been checked in {days} days. Sprint is blocked. To unblock: /pulse run claims-policy

problem_classes.name: Privacy & Data Handling
problem_classes.description: Personal data is collected, stored, and processed lawfully
examples: no way to delete user data
problem_classes.detection: Consent flow audit, data collection inventory, deletion capability check

problem_classes.name: Cookie & Tracking Consent
problem_classes.description: Tracking technologies require user consent where legally mandated
problem_classes.detection: Cookie audit, tracking script inventory, consent flow verification

problem_classes.name: Terms & Legal Documents
problem_classes.description: Required legal documents exist, are accessible, and match actual behavior
examples: privacy policy doesn't mention data sharing with third parties
problem_classes.detection: Document existence check, content-vs-behavior cross-reference

problem_classes.name: Industry-Specific Requirements
problem_classes.description: Regulations specific to the application's domain are met
problem_classes.detection: Domain-specific checklist audit (determined during baseline based on detected industry)

problem_classes.name: Access & Audit Controls
problem_classes.description: Who accessed what data and when is traceable
examples: no audit log for admin actions
no access controls on sensitive data exports
problem_classes.detection: Audit trail review, access control matrix, logging coverage check

on_stale.message: Compliance program has not been checked in {days} days.

problem_classes.name: Input Validation
problem_classes.description: All data entering the system is validated before storage
examples: no length limits on text fields
problem_classes.detection: Validation rule audit per model field, API schema review, form validation check

problem_classes.name: Schema & Migration Safety
problem_classes.description: Database schema changes are safe, reversible, and don't lose data
examples: index missing on frequently queried field
problem_classes.detection: Migration review, schema diff analysis, query plan check

problem_classes.name: Referential Integrity
problem_classes.description: Relationships between data entities are consistent and enforced
examples: foreign key not enforced at DB level
problem_classes.detection: Foreign key audit, orphan record scan, cascade behavior review

problem_classes.name: Data Consistency
problem_classes.description: The same data viewed from different paths shows the same value
problem_classes.detection: Cache invalidation audit, denormalization sync check, aggregate verification

problem_classes.name: Data Loss Prevention
problem_classes.description: Data cannot be silently lost, truncated, or corrupted
problem_classes.detection: Truncation risk audit, concurrent write analysis, backup verification

on_stale.message: Data quality program has not been checked in {days} days.

problem_classes.name: Gate 1 Entry skipped
problem_classes.description: UX-touching change made production without scope classification, persona/JTBD identification, or gap analysis (GREEN/YELLOW/RED verdict). Per CLAUDE.md Design Program Guardrail, Gate 1 is non-bypassable for new views/widgets/destructive actions/layout refactors/visible-copy changes on primary controls.
examples: new SCREEN.* added without gap analysis
destructive action added without persona check
problem_classes.detection: git diff against last sweep, scoped to UX surfaces. Cross-reference each PR description for audit-id or surface-id citation per CLAUDE.md.

problem_classes.name: Gate 2 Blind-Spot skipped
problem_classes.description: Gate 1 returned YELLOW or RED, or the work introduces a new surface, targets an underserved persona, or crosses product lines — but no blind-spot roundtable was conducted before specifying the design. Per docs/design/ROUNDTABLES.md.
examples: YELLOW Gate 1 verdict but no audit doc at docs/design/audits/
problem_classes.detection: Cross-reference Gate 1 verdicts in audit docs against existence of corresponding roundtable doc.

problem_classes.name: Gate 4 Design surface artifact missing
problem_classes.description: UX change in production but no surface artifact exists at docs/design/surfaces/<surface-id>.md. Per CLAUDE.md, this gate is non-bypassable.
examples: new view in src/components/views/ with no surfaces/<view>.md
navigation pattern changed without spec update
problem_classes.detection: Cross-reference docs/design/surfaces/CATALOG.md against SCREEN constants and active routes.

problem_classes.name: PROTO persona staleness
problem_classes.description: Personas marked PROTO (provisional) past 180 days without a verification pass — observation, structured interview, or downgrade to ARCHIVED. Per design.md health metrics.
examples: 15 of 15 personas PROTO baseline 2026-04-21 — no verification by 2026-10-21
problem_classes.detection: Scan docs/design/personas/ for `status: PROTO` files older than 180 days from `last_verified` or file creation.

problem_classes.name: Discovery LOG aging
problem_classes.description: Items in docs/design/discoveries/ stuck in CAPTURED state past 60 days without triage to RESEARCHED or DEFERRED.
examples: DISC-* in CAPTURED state with capture date > 60 days ago
problem_classes.detection: Scan docs/design/discoveries/*.md for status field; flag CAPTURED items past threshold.

problem_classes.name: Surface CATALOG drift
problem_classes.description: docs/design/surfaces/CATALOG.md falls out of sync with active SCREEN.* constants in src/constants/uiConstants.js. New routes added without surface artifacts.
examples: SCREEN.PLAYER_EDITOR added (PEO) but missing from CATALOG
view directory added with no surface entry
problem_classes.detection: Diff SCREEN constants against CATALOG entries; report missing surfaces.

problem_classes.name: JTBD domain unserved
problem_classes.description: JTBD domain referenced by 1+ active discoveries but has 0 served surface artifacts. Tracked as risk, not blocker.
examples: subscription-account JTBD referenced but no served surface
coaching domain demand visible in discoveries with no surfaces
problem_classes.detection: Cross-reference JTBD domain references in docs/design/jtbd/ against surface artifacts.

problem_classes.name: Audit findings open past SLA
problem_classes.description: Audit-generated findings in docs/design/audits/*.md still open > 60 days from audit date.
examples: MPMF audit 2026-04-24 with finding still open 2026-06-25
problem_classes.detection: Scan audit docs for unresolved findings; flag those past SLA.

on_stale.message: Design program has not run a sweep in {days} days. Run /pulse run design.

problem_classes.name: First-principles decision modeling
problem_classes.description: Villain decisions must derive from equity, pot odds, SPR, and players remaining — NEVER from position/bucket/style labels directly. Labels are OUTPUTS of the decision process, not inputs. Stacking style on top of the stats that define style is double-counting.
examples: if (position === 'EP') foldRate *= 1.05  // wrong — derive from per-position pre-flop equity, not the label
POP_CALLING_RATES[bucket] used when per-combo equity available — should use logistic
applying style multiplier on top of villain.vpip + villain.pfr — those stats DEFINE the style, double-counting
problem_classes.detection: grep for hardcoded position/bucket/style label conditionals in src/utils/exploitEngine/, src/utils/rangeEngine/, src/utils/villainDecisionModel/ — cross-reference against POKER_THEORY.md §7 + exploitEngine/CLAUDE.md anti-patterns

problem_classes.name: Range estimation correctness
problem_classes.description: Range engine must use Bayesian methods with population priors, never frequentist methods. Per-combo equity preferred over bucket-anchor approximations when computationally feasible. Sample-size confidence must propagate as credible intervals, not point estimates.
examples: z-test on small-sample VPIP — should be Bayesian credible interval
uniform prior on villain range — should be population prior conditioned on stakes/pool
bucket-anchor equity used in depth-1 game tree where per-combo enumeration was viable
problem_classes.detection: Audit src/utils/rangeEngine/* for prior/posterior shape, sample-size handling, credible-interval propagation. Cross-reference RANGE_ENGINE_DESIGN.md.

problem_classes.name: Equity calculation correctness
problem_classes.description: Equity computation must be Monte Carlo with sufficient samples or exact enumeration when ≤20 combos. Stratified sampling for flush-bearing turn cards. Texture-aware adjustments to equity realization (wet OOP ×0.92, dry OOP ×1.05).
examples: Equity rollout with too-few samples for required confidence band
Missing exact enumeration fast-path on small ranges
problem_classes.detection: Audit src/utils/handAnalysis/equityCalc.js, computeRiverCheckEV, computeRiverBetEV, exactEnumerateEquity. Cross-reference POKER_THEORY.md §5.

problem_classes.name: Game tree EV correctness
problem_classes.description: Game tree must compute depth-2/3 EV correctly with rake threading, polarization sizing, blocker effects, multi-way fold correlation, and 5-zone SPR strategy. Action-rate-weighted sub-range equity for filtered actions. Bet-sizing range shapes (air/value/draw/capped/polarized).
examples: depth-2 raise response disabled (enableDepth2Raise=false) when budget allows
Rake not threaded through foldEquityCalc → depth-2/3
Multi-way fold correlation missing in 3+ player pots
Polarization sizing missing for river overbet
problem_classes.detection: Audit src/utils/gameTreeEvaluator.js, src/utils/villainDecisionModel/, src/utils/foldEquityCalc.js. Verify rake threading via potCalculator. Cross-reference POKER_THEORY.md §6 + memory: project_game_tree_*.

problem_classes.name: Bayesian confidence representation
problem_classes.description: Sample-size-aware confidence must propagate as credible intervals (Bayesian) not point estimates (frequentist). All decision-display surfaces must reflect uncertainty (worthiness, dataQuality, confidence-weighted display).
examples: Point-estimate fold% surfaced where credible interval is computed upstream
Action recommendation displayed without sample-size caveat
Confidence band not narrowing as samples accumulate
problem_classes.detection: Audit src/utils/bayesianConfidence.js + downstream consumers (PlayerAnalysisPanel, OnlineView, HandReplay). Verify credible interval bounds reach UI.

problem_classes.name: Villain decision model fidelity
problem_classes.description: Villain model must use style-conditioned priors (Fish/Nit/LAG/TAG override population), per-bucket differential scaling (calling station inflates marginals, not air uniformly), logistic fold response (not linear), street-conditional depth-2 rates, texture preservation through L3 of hierarchy.
examples: Linear fold% scaling on bet size
Style applied as multiplier on top of vpip/pfr — double-counted
Texture dropped at L2 of villain model hierarchy
Multiway model treats sequential decisions as independent
problem_classes.detection: Audit src/utils/villainDecisionModel/. Cross-reference memory: project_villain_model_assumptions_fix.md.

problem_classes.name: Exploit generation soundness
problem_classes.description: Exploits must derive from game-tree EV computation, not from hardcoded weakness-label maps. Weaknesses (villain behavior observations) ≠ exploits (hero recommendations). Current WEAKNESS_EXPLOIT_MAP is a known stopgap awaiting Phase 4D removal.
examples: New exploit added via WEAKNESS_EXPLOIT_MAP entry instead of game tree
Hero action recommended without game-tree EV justification
Same weakness producing same exploit across all situations (situation-blind)
problem_classes.detection: Audit src/utils/exploitEngine/generateExploits.js. Check WEAKNESS_EXPLOIT_MAP usage — should be shrinking, not growing. Cross-reference memory: feedback_weakness_exploit_separation.md.

problem_classes.name: Theory-spec drift
problem_classes.description: POKER_THEORY.md must remain the canonical doctrine. Engine implementations must not silently diverge from it. ADR required for any deliberate divergence.
examples: Engine uses formula X but POKER_THEORY.md states formula Y
New algorithm added without theory-doc update
problem_classes.detection: Grep engine implementations for formulas/thresholds; cross-reference against POKER_THEORY.md content. Stale-date check on `last verified` line.

problem_classes.name: Anti-pattern enforcement
problem_classes.description: Anti-patterns enumerated in src/utils/exploitEngine/CLAUDE.md and src/utils/rangeEngine/CLAUDE.md must not appear in committed code.
examples: Conflating calling station with weak range
Treating bet sizing as deterministic of range shape (without showdown calibration)
problem_classes.detection: Re-read sub-directory CLAUDE.md anti-pattern sections; grep for the anti-patterns in committed code.

on_stale.message: Poker Theory Integrity has not been checked in {days} days. Sprint is blocked. To unblock: /pulse run domain-correctness

problem_classes.name: Test Coverage & Quality
problem_classes.description: Tests exist, are meaningful, and cover critical paths
examples: tests that pass but don't assert anything
problem_classes.detection: Coverage analysis, test file audit, assertion density check

problem_classes.name: Architecture Drift
problem_classes.description: Code structure diverges from intended patterns over time
problem_classes.detection: Dependency analysis, module boundary checks, file size trends

problem_classes.name: Dependency Health
problem_classes.description: Third-party packages are current, secure, and necessary
problem_classes.detection: Package audit, unused import scan, version freshness check

problem_classes.name: Error Handling & Resilience
problem_classes.description: Code handles failures gracefully, not just the happy path
examples: no retry logic on external calls
problem_classes.detection: Exception handling audit, external call patterns, logging coverage

problem_classes.name: Build & Deploy Pipeline
problem_classes.description: CI/CD works, is fast, and catches problems before production
problem_classes.detection: CI config review, deploy script audit, pipeline timing analysis

problem_classes.name: Code Maintainability
problem_classes.description: Code is understandable and changeable by the next session's AI
problem_classes.detection: Complexity metrics, naming analysis, coupling detection

on_stale.message: Engineering program has not been checked in {days} days.

problem_classes.name: Deployment Safety
problem_classes.description: Deploys are reliable, reversible, and don't break production
examples: migrations run before code is ready
problem_classes.detection: CI/CD pipeline audit, rollback mechanism check, deployment step review

problem_classes.name: Availability & Recovery
problem_classes.description: Application recovers from failures without data loss
problem_classes.detection: Health endpoint check, redundancy audit, backup test, recovery runbook review

problem_classes.name: Monitoring & Alerting
problem_classes.description: Problems are detected before users report them
examples: alerts go to an unmonitored channel
problem_classes.detection: Monitoring tool inventory, alert routing verification, coverage gap analysis

problem_classes.name: Scaling & Performance
problem_classes.description: Application handles load growth without degradation
problem_classes.detection: Query analysis, resource utilization patterns, caching layer audit

problem_classes.name: Cost & Resource Management
problem_classes.description: Infrastructure costs are reasonable and won't surprise the founder
problem_classes.detection: Resource inventory, cost trend analysis, orphaned resource scan

on_stale.message: Infrastructure program has not been checked in {days} days.

problem_classes.name: Blocking Program Health
problem_classes.description: All programs that must be healthy before launch are actually healthy
examples: security program has open critical findings
problem_classes.detection: Cross-program health check against launch_gate.blocking_programs

problem_classes.name: Core User Flows
problem_classes.description: Primary user journeys work end-to-end
problem_classes.detection: Critical path enumeration and verification

problem_classes.name: Operational Readiness
problem_classes.description: Monitoring, alerting, and incident response are in place
problem_classes.detection: Monitoring coverage audit, alerting rule review, runbook existence check

on_stale.message: Launch readiness has not been assessed in {days} days. Sprint is blocked. To unblock: /pulse run launch-readiness

problem_classes.name: Scoring/Classification Bias
problem_classes.description: The method of scoring or classifying produces systematically skewed results
problem_classes.detection: Bias audit, calibration check, sensitivity analysis on methodology parameters

problem_classes.name: Sample Size & Statistical Validity
problem_classes.description: Decisions are made on insufficient data, or confidence exceeds what the data supports
problem_classes.detection: Sample size verification, confidence calibration check, prior sensitivity analysis

problem_classes.name: Post-Hoc Fitting & Flexibility
problem_classes.description: Results look good because the method was tuned to match known answers
problem_classes.detection: Degrees of freedom audit, search space enumeration, blind prediction verification

problem_classes.name: Cross-Method Consistency
problem_classes.description: Different methods applied to the same question produce different answers
examples: two calculation paths give different totals
problem_classes.detection: Multi-path verification, reconciliation check, aggregate-vs-detail comparison

problem_classes.name: Methodology Drift
problem_classes.description: The method changes over time without tracking, invalidating comparisons
examples: classification criteria evolved without re-scoring old items
threshold changed but historical data not re-evaluated
problem_classes.detection: Methodology version tracking, change log audit, historical consistency check

on_stale.message: Methodology integrity program has not been checked in {days} days. Decision methods may be producing unreliable results.

problem_classes.name: Authentication & Authorization
problem_classes.description: Who can access what, and is that enforced correctly everywhere
problem_classes.detection: Route/endpoint audit for auth decorators, role-check coverage, session management review

problem_classes.name: Data Protection
problem_classes.description: Sensitive data is encrypted, masked, and never exposed inappropriately
problem_classes.detection: Log content scan, database field audit, API response schema review

problem_classes.name: Secrets Management
problem_classes.description: API keys, passwords, tokens are never in code and are rotated
problem_classes.detection: Secret pattern grep, git history scan for leaked secrets, CI log review

problem_classes.name: Input Validation & Injection
problem_classes.description: All user input is validated and cannot be used to attack the system
examples: command injection via file upload names
problem_classes.detection: Input handling audit, parameterized query check, output encoding verification

problem_classes.name: Dependency Vulnerabilities
problem_classes.description: Third-party packages have no known security vulnerabilities
problem_classes.detection: Package audit tools, CVE database cross-reference, maintenance status check

problem_classes.name: Infrastructure & Configuration
problem_classes.description: Server, cloud, and deployment configs follow security best practices
examples: no rate limiting on auth endpoints
problem_classes.detection: Config file audit, header analysis, rate limit verification

on_stale.message: Security program has not been checked in {days} days. Sprint is blocked. To unblock: /pulse run security

problem_classes.name: Principle Violation
problem_classes.description: A constitutional principle (P1, P2, P3) is detectably violated
examples: P2 violated — a command asks founder to validate YAML
P3 violated — /adopt requires M1-M5 completion upfront
problem_classes.detection: constitutional-audit engine; kit/scripts/cwos-constitutional-audit.js detectPN() functions

problem_classes.name: Founder-Invariant Breach
problem_classes.description: INV-F1 (No Unnecessary Burden) fails — installed components are not proportional to goal value
examples: 2+ installed programs with no goal-alignment trace
component-alignment engine scores >=2 components at <=2/10 relevance
problem_classes.detection: constitutional-audit detectInvF1; component-alignment engine low-relevance threshold

problem_classes.name: Failed State Detected
problem_classes.description: A concrete dystopia from intention.md is firing in the current repo
examples: FS-1 ceremony theater — rituals without subsequent action
FS-7 findings noise — engine run produced >=15 findings
FS-10 self-aggrandizing complexity — programs lack contract trace to intention.md
problem_classes.detection: constitutional-audit detectFSN functions per failed state

problem_classes.name: Detector Coverage Gap
problem_classes.description: A constitutional item has no detector or its detector is infeasible — the constitution is declared but not watched
examples: FS-6 infeasible — detector regex mismatches current queue shape
problem_classes.detection: constitutional-audit flags status: infeasible or no-detector per item; each promotes to a LOW finding

on_stale.message: Self-compliance has not been checked in {days} days. The constitution is declared but unwatched. To unblock: /pulse run self-compliance
